title: Docs Viewer Modal Consistency
summary: Give every Docs Viewer management modal one shared lifecycle and a deliberate set of styling variants without absorbing workflow logic.
content_html:
Docs Viewer Modal Consistency

Status

Complete. Every current Docs Viewer management modal now uses one shared lifecycle, deliberate presentation variants cover the retained families, and the permanent Admin-hosted Workbench provides production-backed specimens for rapid tactile review.

Purpose

Make Docs Viewer modals behave and feel like one product while keeping each workflow's domain state, validation, requests, and results with its existing owner.

This is a maintenance feature, not a new modal framework or a general visual redesign.

Dependency

Docs Viewer Manage Theme Restoration closes first. It owns the manage route's theme-token regression and initial dark presentation across current overlays. This feature then uses that working baseline to converge modal lifecycle, presentation, and implementation ownership.

Delivered Boundary

Docs Viewer currently has two modal composition paths:

  • the shared ephemeral management shell used by scope lifecycle, document packages, copy, creation, source-editor, media, confirmation, choice, and notice workflows;
  • persistent Metadata, Import, and Settings markup with a separate lifecycle controller.

The two composition paths retain their appropriate rendering ownership but now consume one lifecycle contract. Shared modal styling owns sizing, action order, scroll behavior, disabled/status treatment, and focus indication; workflow-specific bodies and state remain with their existing owners.

The Workbench initially exposed an unwired Review Sessions implementation left behind when temporary in-viewer sessions were replaced by the separate Docs Review application. It was preparatory code rather than a current modal path and was retired during DMC-P2.

Direction

  • Keep one Docs Viewer-owned modal lifecycle contract for initial focus, focus containment, Escape and backdrop handling, page-scroll locking, focus restoration, and keyboard-focus indication.
  • Let that lifecycle manage both dynamically rendered modal frames and persistent workflow-owned markup.
  • Keep explicit compact, standard, document, import, and wide presentation variants. Consistency does not require identical dimensions or initial-focus targets.
  • Standardize action order, disabled treatment, status states, stale-status clearing, body/footer overflow ownership, responsive fit, and light/dark presentation.
  • Treat the restored manage theme tokens as an input; do not reopen foundational route theming inside modal-consistency checkpoints.
  • Keep domain workflow bodies and state local. The shared modal owner must not learn about scopes, packages, imports, metadata, media, or service endpoints.
  • Contribute a permanent Docs Viewer specimen pack to the federated UI Workbench so every meaningful modal state can be inspected without executing the real workflow.
  • Use the workbench to classify apparently equivalent controls by interaction contract before sharing implementations. Studio participation and remediation remain a separate delivery.
  • Use focused automated evidence for the shared lifecycle and browser integration boundaries. Use temporary or manual browser inspection for visual fit, focus feel, pointer behaviour, copy, and narrow-screen ergonomics.

The narrow Workbench condition is constrained desktop-browser layout evidence, not a claim that the Python-backed local applications support mobile deployment. Mobile use of the genuinely public reader is the separate Public Docs Viewer Mobile Experience feature.

Feature Documents

  • UI Workbench Concept — intention, federated ownership, production-backed specimens, comparison method, and maintenance triggers.
  • Modal Consistency Delivery — proposed audit, lifecycle convergence, styling correction, migration, and verification.
  • Docs Viewer Roadmap — scheduling and dependency context.

Outcome

Every current Docs Viewer modal uses the same lifecycle owner, deliberate style variants cover every retained family, workflow-local behavior is preserved, and meaningful reachable states are available through the permanent production-backed Workbench pack. Focused evidence and user-approved tactile review cover desktop, constrained-width, light, dark, keyboard, pointer, long-content, busy, validation, and terminal-result behavior.

The exact modal inventory remains code-owned. Do not turn this feature page into a permanent selector or workflow catalogue.
